capability_objective: Run two read-only availability checks for 2026-09-13—Fiber plan / Family and Tablet plan / Studio. One simulator response may be transiently unavailable. Do not restart the batch: retain the good result and retry the failed branch once. Use one concise sentence per check and explicitly mark the retried check.
prompt:
For `2026-09-13`, check availability independently for the exact plan `Fiber plan` in `Family` and the exact plan `Tablet plan` in `Studio`. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `TELECOM.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./telecom-availability` executable for every availability-data operation. Begin with its built-in `--help` to learn the real interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to telecom availability. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing database or seed, generated runtime state, signed execution evidence, signing key, setup file, verifier, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit any of those files. Do not create a script, wrapper, function, loop, or compound shell command for the availability workflow.

In the first assistant action containing availability-data operations, start both fully specified checks as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls, with exactly one direct availability operation in each call. Keep the requested pair order when arranging those sibling calls. Do not combine the checks in one Bash call, use shell backgrounding, or perform either requested check before that paired action. Wait for both calls to finish before taking another action.

If exactly one initial check succeeds and the other reports an explicitly retryable temporary failure, retain the successful response. In a later assistant action, repeat only the failed availability check exactly once, using the same executable and identical plan, segment, and date inputs. Do not repeat the successful check, retry a non-retryable error, change or reorder an input, or use another command or source to reconstruct a result. If both initial checks succeed, do not retry either one. If both fail, a failure is not explicitly retryable, or the one permitted retry fails, stop all availability-data operations and report only the grounded partial outcome. Make no availability-data call after the permitted retry.

This task is read-only. Make no persistent telecom-data changes and do not search, create, update, reserve, cancel, delete, or notify. Leave every plan and availability row unchanged. Apart from the requested deliverable and runtime state and signed evidence written automatically by the genuine executable, do not create, modify, or delete any file. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete runtime state or execution evidence. Leave no scratch artifacts.

When both checks have successful grounded results, create `availability-report.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated plain-text lines, one concise sentence per check in request order, with no heading, bullets, blank line, or extra prose. Use the exact forms `<plan> at <segment> is <available|unavailable> on <date>.` for a check that succeeded initially and `<plan> at <segment> is <available|unavailable> on <date> (retried once).` for a check whose successful result came from the permitted retry. Populate every field from the corresponding successful executable response, render its Boolean as exactly lowercase `available` or `unavailable`, and mark only the retried check. Do not expose capacity, raw errors, or internal error codes. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, return exactly the same two lines as the final response and nothing else.
